_meta.description: Catalog for the in-session structured assessment panel (Phase 3 of the scribe context upgrades). Each measure's `key` MUST equal a test key in normative-data.json so the deterministic engine (normative-data.interpretByKey) grades the captured value against age/sex norms — exactly the same grounding the handout uses, but from a tapped number rather than text extracted from speech. The UI is fully driven by this file: adding an assessment is data, not code. Assessments are grouped by `category` in the picker.

laterality.bilateral: Capture a left and a right value (each stored as its own row). The norm is not side-specific; a >10% side-to-side gap is flagged at grading.
laterality.single: One value for the whole body (stored with side='bilateral'). Use for non-lateralised tests.

input.presets: Tablet-fast: tappable preset buttons from min→max in `presetStep` increments (e.g. ROM eyeballed to the nearest 10°). Tapping commits and advances.
input.keypad: On-screen numeric keypad for an exact instrument read (dynamometer kg, stopwatch seconds, counts, mmol/L).
input.compound: Two numbers stored as value + value2 (e.g. blood pressure systolic/diastolic). `min/max` bound the first, `min2/max2` the second.
input.toggle: Pass/fail or positive/negative observation. `options` lists {value,label}; the captured value is stored numerically and rendered as its label.
input.instrument: Multi-item scored protocol (Berg, Mini-BEST). The assessment carries an `instrument` block with `items[]` (each: name, instruction, anchored `options`, optional `bilateral`/`section`). The clinician scores item-by-item in a guided runner; the server sums the total (worse side for bilateral items), stores it in `value` (graded vs the norm key) with the per-item breakdown in `detail`.

_meta.note: A measure may carry its own `laterality` to override the assessment's (e.g. cervical: flexion/extension single, rotation/lateral-flexion bilateral).

instrument.attribution: © 2005–2013 Oregon Health & Science University. Free for clinical use with attribution (bestest.us).

items.name: Sit to stand
items.instruction: Cross your arms; try not to use your hands. Please stand up now.

options.label: Normal: stands without hands, stabilises independently
Moderate: stands WITH hands on first attempt
Severe: unable without assistance / several attempts

items.name: Rise to toes
items.instruction: Hands on hips, feet shoulder width. Rise as high as you can onto your toes; hold ≥3 seconds.

options.label: Normal: stable 3 s at maximum height
Moderate: not full range, or unstable for 3 s
Severe: <3 seconds

items.name: Stand on one leg
items.instruction: Hands on hips, lift one leg behind you and hold as long as you can (best of 2 trials per side).

options.label: Normal: 20 seconds
Moderate: <20 seconds
Severe: unable

items.name: Compensatory stepping — forward
items.instruction: Lean forward against my hands beyond your limits; when I release, step to avoid a fall.

options.label: Normal: recovers with a single, large step
Moderate: more than one step to recover
Severe: no step / would fall / falls spontaneously

items.name: Compensatory stepping — backward
items.instruction: Lean backward against my hands beyond your limits; when I release, step to avoid a fall.

options.label: Normal: recovers with a single, large step
Moderate: more than one step to recover
Severe: no step / would fall / falls spontaneously

items.name: Compensatory stepping — lateral
items.instruction: Feet together; lean into my hand beyond your sideways limit; when I release, step to avoid a fall.

options.label: Normal: recovers with 1 step (crossover or lateral)
Moderate: several steps to recover
Severe: falls or cannot step

items.name: Stance: eyes open, firm surface
items.instruction: Hands on hips, feet together, eyes open. Stay as still as possible (up to 30 s).

options.label: Normal: 30 seconds
Moderate: <30 seconds
Severe: unable

items.name: Stance: eyes closed, foam
items.instruction: On the foam, hands on hips, feet together, eyes closed. Stay as still as possible (up to 30 s).

options.label: Normal: 30 seconds
Moderate: <30 seconds
Severe: unable

items.name: Incline, eyes closed
items.instruction: Stand on the incline ramp, toes toward the top, arms at sides, eyes closed (up to 30 s).

options.label: Normal: 30 s, aligns with gravity
Moderate: <30 s OR aligns with surface
Severe: unable

items.name: Change in gait speed
items.instruction: Walk at normal speed; on 'fast' walk as fast as you can, on 'slow' walk very slowly.

options.label: Normal: changes speed significantly, no imbalance
Moderate: unable to change speed OR signs of imbalance
Severe: no significant change AND signs of imbalance

items.name: Walk with head turns — horizontal
items.instruction: Walk at normal speed; turn your head right/left on command, keep walking straight.

options.label: Normal: head turns, no change in speed, good balance
Moderate: head turns with reduction in gait speed
Severe: head turns with imbalance

items.name: Walk with pivot turns
items.instruction: Walk at normal speed; on 'turn and stop', turn quickly, face the opposite way, feet close together.

options.label: Normal: turns feet-close FAST (<3 steps), good balance
Moderate: turns feet-close SLOW (≥4 steps), good balance
Severe: cannot turn feet-close without imbalance

items.name: Step over obstacles
items.instruction: Walk at normal speed; at the box, step over it (not around) and keep walking.

options.label: Normal: steps over box, minimal speed change, good balance
Moderate: steps over but touches box OR slows cautiously
Severe: unable to step over OR steps around box

items.name: Timed Up & Go with dual task
items.instruction: TUG while counting backwards by threes. Compare with TUG alone (decrease a point if gait slows >10%).

options.label: Normal: no noticeable change vs TUG without dual task
Moderate: dual task affects counting OR walking (>10%)
Severe: stops counting while walking, or stops walking

instrument.attribution: Berg K et al. 1989. Public-domain clinical scale.

items.name: Sitting to standing
items.instruction: Please stand up. Try not to use your hands for support.

options.label: Stands, no hands, stabilises independently
Stands independently using hands
Stands using hands after several tries
Needs minimal aid to stand or stabilise
Needs moderate or maximal assist

items.name: Standing unsupported
items.instruction: Please stand for two minutes without holding on.

options.label: Stands safely 2 minutes
Stands 2 minutes with supervision
Stands 30 seconds unsupported
Needs several tries to stand 30 seconds
Unable to stand 30 seconds unsupported

items.name: Sitting unsupported
items.instruction: Please sit with arms folded for two minutes (back unsupported, feet supported).

options.label: Sits safely and securely 2 minutes
Sits 2 minutes under supervision
Sits 30 seconds
Sits 10 seconds
Unable to sit without support 10 seconds

items.name: Standing to sitting
items.instruction: Please sit down.

options.label: Sits safely with minimal use of hands
Controls descent by using hands
Uses back of legs against chair to control descent
Sits independently but uncontrolled descent
Needs assist to sit

items.name: Transfers
items.instruction: Transfer one way toward a seat with armrests and one way toward a seat without armrests.

options.label: Transfers safely, minor use of hands
Transfers safely, definite need of hands
Transfers with verbal cueing and/or supervision
Needs one person to assist
Needs two people to assist or supervise

items.name: Standing with eyes closed
items.instruction: Please close your eyes and stand still for 10 seconds.

options.label: Stands 10 seconds safely
Stands 10 seconds with supervision
Stands 3 seconds
Unable to keep eyes closed 3 s but stays safely
Needs help to keep from falling

items.name: Standing with feet together
items.instruction: Place your feet together and stand without holding on.

options.label: Feet together independently, stands 1 minute safely
Feet together independently, 1 minute with supervision
Feet together independently but <30 seconds
Needs help to position but stands 15 s feet together
Needs help to position, unable to hold 15 seconds

items.name: Reaching forward with outstretched arm
items.instruction: Lift arm to 90°, stretch your fingers and reach forward as far as you can.

options.label: Reaches forward confidently >25 cm (10 in)
Reaches forward >12 cm (5 in)
Reaches forward >5 cm (2 in)
Reaches forward but needs supervision
Loses balance / requires external support

items.name: Retrieving object from floor
items.instruction: Pick up the shoe/slipper in front of your feet.

options.label: Picks up slipper safely and easily
Picks up slipper but needs supervision
Reaches 2–5 cm from slipper, keeps balance
Unable to pick up, needs supervision while trying
Unable / needs assist to keep from falling

items.name: Turning to look behind
items.instruction: Turn to look directly behind you over the left shoulder, then the right.

options.label: Looks behind both sides, good weight shift
Looks behind one side only, less shift other side
Turns sideways only but maintains balance
Needs supervision when turning
Needs assist to keep from falling

items.name: Turning 360 degrees
items.instruction: Turn completely around in a full circle, pause, then turn the other way.

options.label: Turns 360° safely in ≤4 seconds each way
Turns 360° safely one side only ≤4 seconds
Turns 360° safely but slowly
Needs close supervision or verbal cueing
Needs assistance while turning

items.name: Placing alternate foot on stool
items.instruction: Place each foot alternately on the step/stool until each foot has touched four times.

options.label: Stands independently, 8 steps in 20 seconds
Stands independently, 8 steps in >20 seconds
Completes 4 steps without aid, with supervision
Completes >2 steps, needs minimal assist
Needs assistance / unable to try

items.name: Standing with one foot in front
items.instruction: Place one foot directly in front of the other (tandem); if not possible, step ahead.

options.label: Foot tandem independently, holds 30 seconds
Foot ahead independently, holds 30 seconds
Small step independently, holds 30 seconds
Needs help to step but holds 15 seconds
Loses balance while stepping or standing

items.name: Standing on one leg
items.instruction: Stand on one leg as long as you can without holding on.

options.label: Lifts leg independently, holds >10 seconds
Lifts leg independently, holds 5–10 seconds
Lifts leg independently, holds ≥3 seconds
Tries to lift, <3 s but stays standing independently
Unable / needs assist to prevent fall
Negative (full elevation)
Positive (restricted)
Normal control
Winging / dyskinesis
